LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF CHI-DONG HWANG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT CONTINUES THE MATTER FOR HEARING TO DECEMBER 18, 2025. IN LIGHT OF THE LIBERAL STANDARD FOR PERMITTING AMENDED PLEADINGS, THE COURT IS INCLINED TO GRANT PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO FILE THE AMENDED PLEADING ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A TO THE BHATT REPLY DECLARATION. BUT BECAUSE THE REVISION CAME IN ON REPLY, DEFENDANTS SHOULD BE GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO OBJECT OR BRIEF THE ISSUE. DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A SURREPLY BY FIVE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF CHI-DONG HWANG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO FILE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT A TO THE BHATT REPLY DECLARATION WITHIN ONE WEEK OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHI-DONG HWANG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE To File First Amended Complaint.

The motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted. Plaintiff has leave to file the first amended complaint attached as Exhibit A to the Bhatt reply declaration within one week of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Continued To Dec-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHI-DONG HWANG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE To File First Amended Complaint.

The court continues the matter for hearing to December 18, 2025. In light of the liberal standard for permitting amended pleadings, the court is inclined to grant plaintiff leave to file the amended pleading attached as Exhibit A to the Bhatt Reply Declaration. But because the revision came in on reply, defendants should be given the opportunity to object or brief the issue. Defendants may file a surreply by five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
